debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
β : Type u_2
G : Graph α β
h : (x : α) → @Graph.inessential α β G x
⊢ ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ ∀ (H : Graph α β), H.IsCompOf G → ∃! x, x ∈ H.vertexSet ∧ x ∉ V(G, M)

failed_attempts:
have h₁ : ∃ (M : Finset β), G.IsMatching M := by
    classical
    -- Use the empty set as a trivial matching
    refine' ⟨∅, _⟩
    simp [Graph.isMatching_iff]
    <;> exact ⟨fun e => fun he => Finset.not_mem_empty e he, fun e f => by simp⟩
  obtain ⟨M, hM⟩ := h₁
  refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
  intro H hH
  have h₂ : H.IsCompOf G := hH
  have h₃ : ∃! x, x ∈ H.vertexSet ∧ x ∉ V(G, M) := by
    classical
    -- Use the fact that H is a component of G and all vertices are inessential to find a unique x
    by_cases hx : ∃ x, x ∈ H.vertexSet ∧ x ∉ V(G, M)
    · -- Case: There exists an x in H that is not in M
      obtain ⟨x, hx₁, hx₂⟩ := hx
      refine' ⟨x, ⟨hx₁, hx₂⟩, _⟩
      intro y ⟨hy₁, hy₂⟩
      -- Use the fact that all vertices are inessential to show uniqueness
      have h₄ := h x
      have h₅ := h y
      have h₆ := h₄
      have h₇ := h₅
      simp [Graph.inessential] at h₄ h₅ h₆ h₇
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Set.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try {
        tauto
      })
    · -- Case: No such x exists
      exfalso
      -- This case should not happen because of the given conditions
      push_neg at hx
      have h₄ := h (Classical.arbitrary α)
      simp [Graph.inessential] at h₄
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Set.ext_iff]) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try {
        tauto
      })
  exact h₃

have h₁ : ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ ∀ H : Graph α β, H.IsCompOf G → ∃! x, x ∈ V(H) ∧ x ∉ V(G, M) := by
    classical
    -- We can choose the empty matching since it is always a matching
    refine' ⟨∅, by simp [Graph.IsMatching], _⟩
    intro H hH
    -- Since H is a component of G, we can find a unique x in H that is not in V(G, ∅)
    have h₂ : ∃! x, x ∈ V(H) ∧ x ∉ V(G, ∅) := by
      -- We use the fact that H is a component and the empty matching to find x
      -- Here, we can choose any element of H since V(G, ∅) is empty
      -- and H is nonempty because it is a component of G
      have h₃ : ∃ x, x ∈ V(H) := by
        -- Since H is a component of G, it must have at least one vertex
        have h₄ : H.IsComponentOf G := hH
        have h₅ : Nonempty α := by
          -- We assume that G is nonempty, which is not explicitly stated but is common in graph theory
          by_cases h₆ : Nonempty α
          · exact h₆
          · exfalso
            simp_all [Graph.IsComponentOf, isEmpty_iff, Set.eq_empty_iff_forall_not_mem]
            <;>
            aesop
        cases' h₅ with a
        have h₆ : a ∈ V(H) := by
          -- a is in V(H) because H is a component of G
          have h₇ : H.IsComponentOf G := hH
          simp_all [Graph.IsComponentOf, Set.eq_empty_iff_forall_not_mem]
          <;>
          aesop
        exact ⟨a, h₆⟩
      obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₃
      refine' ⟨x, ⟨hx, by simp [V]⟩, _⟩
      rintro y ⟨hy, hy'⟩
      -- Since V(G, ∅) is empty, we can choose any element of H
      -- and uniqueness is guaranteed by the properties of the component
      simp_all [V]
      <;>
      aesop
    exact h₂
  exact h₁
